metadata.name: lhs_2016_6param
metadata.description:
Latin Hypercube Sampling sweep at 2016-block retarget expanding to 6 active parameters.

PURPOSE: Mirror of lhs_144_6param at 2016-block retarget. The existing
lhs_2016_full_parameter (4D, n=64) did not vary pool_profitability_threshold
or solo_miner_hashrate. This sweep supersedes it for feature importance purposes
by including those dimensions.

At 2016-block retarget, pool_committed_split is already confirmed as the dominant
variable (separation=0.275 vs next-best 0.059 in the 4D LHS). This 6D sweep tests:
  1. Whether pool_profitability_threshold shifts the committed_split threshold
     (if pools require more profit to switch, the flip-point may move)
  2. Whether solo_miner_hashrate affects the v26/v27 boundary at 2016-block
     (structural asymmetry may be more or less load-bearing than at 144-block)
  3. Provides a clean 6D regime comparison with lhs_144_6param

DESIGN:
- 130 scenarios via Latin Hypercube Sampling (26 per namespace, 5 namespaces)
- Same 6 parameters and ranges as lhs_144_6param for direct regime comparison
- 2016-block retarget for realistic Bitcoin mainnet dynamics
- Duration 13000s

RUNTIME: 130 scenarios x ~35 min = ~76 hours sequential
         With 5 namespaces: ~15.2 hours

GENERATES: Unbiased 2016-block feature importance across 6D parameter space.
           Supersedes lhs_2016_full_parameter for regime comparison purposes.
           Direct comparison with lhs_144_6param confirms or refutes regime shift
           in dominant causal variable (pool_committed_split at 2016 vs
           economic_split at 144).
